articles.title: These types of breakups tend to coincide with moving on more easily
articles.titleKo: 이러한 유형의 헤어짐은 더 쉽게 다음 단계로 넘어가는 경향이 있습니다
articles.summary: Scientists have found an association between honest, understanding breakups and feeling more energized afterward. While supportive communication doesn't reduce immediate sadness, it tends to coincide with a better overall outlook for everyone involved.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates factors that influence psychological recovery following romantic breakups. Scientists examined participants who recently experienced breakups to analyze the relationship between communication styles during separation and subsequent emotional states. The key finding is that when couples employ honest and understanding communication during breakups, participants tend to feel more energized and maintain a more positive outlook. Notably, while supportive communication did not immediately reduce sadness in the aftermath, it showed a strong association with long-term psychological recovery and positive attitudes for all parties involved. This research suggests that how people communicate during the difficult experience of breakup significantly impacts individual resilience. However, as this study demonstrates correlation rather than causation, further research is needed to determine whether specific communication styles necessarily produce better outcomes.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 헤어짐 이후 심리적 회복에 영향을 미치는 요인을 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 최근 헤어짐을 경험한 참가자들을 대상으로 헤어짐 과정에서의 의사소통 방식과 그 이후 감정 상태 간의 관계를 분석했습니다. 핵심 발견은 솔직하고 이해심 있는 의사소통을 통해 헤어진 경우, 참가자들이 더욱 활기차고 긍정적인 전망을 가지는 경향이 있다는 것입니다. 흥미롭게도 지지적인 의사소통이 직후의 슬픔을 즉각적으로 줄이지는 못했지만, 장기적인 심리적 회복과 긍정적 태도와는 강한 연관성을 보였습니다. 이 연구는 헤어짐이라는 어려운 상황에서도 어떻게 대화하는지가 개인의 회복력에 중요하다는 것을 시사합니다. 다만 이 연구는 상관관계를 보여주는 것으로, 특정 의사소통 방식이 반드시 더 나은 결과를 초래하는지는 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigates factors that influence psychological recovery following romantic breakups. Scientists examined participants who recently experienced breakups to analyze the relationship between communication styles during separation and subsequent emotional states. The key finding is that when couples employ honest and understanding communication during breakups, participants tend to feel more energized and maintain a more positive outlook. Notably, while supportive communication did not immediately reduce sadness in the aftermath, it showed a strong association with long-term psychological recovery and positive attitudes for all parties involved. This research suggests that how people communicate during the difficult experience of breakup significantly impacts individual resilience. However, as this study demonstrates correlation rather than causation, further research is needed to determine whether specific communication styles necessarily produce better outcomes.

##
keyPoints: **솔직하고 이해심 있는 소통의 중요성** / Importance of honest and understanding communication during breakups
**장기적 회복과의 연관성** / Association with long-term psychological recovery and positive outlook
**즉각적 슬픔 감소 효과 없음** / Does not reduce immediate sadness but supports overall emotional well-being
**모든 관계자의 심리적 안녕에 영향** / Affects psychological well-being for everyone involved in the breakup

articles.title: Why thinking hard feels bad: the emotional root of deliberation
articles.titleKo: 왜 깊이 생각하는 것은 힘들까: 숙고의 감정적 근원
articles.summary: Doubt is not just a lack of information; it is an unpleasant emotional state. New psychological research reveals that this negative feeling serves as a necessary alarm bell, pushing the human brain to abandon mental shortcuts and think critically.
articles.aiSummary:
This research reveals that doubt and uncertainty are not merely a lack of information but rather an unpleasant emotional state. Psychologists discovered that the negative feeling we experience when thinking deeply about difficult problems is actually an important signal mechanism. The research team presented participants with cognitive tasks of varying difficulty and measured their emotional responses. Results showed that when people abandoned intuitive thinking (mental shortcuts) and shifted to critical thinking, they experienced discomfort—and this emotion drove more careful and accurate judgments. The findings suggest that doubt and anxiety are not obstacles but evolutionarily useful signaling systems. However, the study was conducted in controlled laboratory settings, so additional research is needed to verify its applicability in real-world, everyday situations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 의심과 불확실성이 단순한 정보 부족이 아니라 불쾌한 정감 상태라는 점을 밝혀냈다. 심리학자들은 우리가 어려운 문제를 깊이 있게 생각할 때 느끼는 부정적 감정이 실제로는 중요한 신호라는 것을 발견했다. 연구팀은 참가자들에게 다양한 난이도의 인지 과제를 제시하고 그들의 정서 반응을 측정했다. 결과적으로 사람들이 직관적 사고(정신적 지름길)를 포기하고 비판적 사고로 전환할 때 불쾌감을 느꼈으며, 이러한 감정이 더 신중하고 정확한 판단을 유도했다. 이 발견은 의심과 불안감이 단순한 장애물이 아니라 진화적으로 유용한 신호 체계임을 시사한다. 다만 이 연구는 실험실 환경에서의 제한된 상황에서 수행되었으므로, 실제 일상생활에서의 적용 가능성을 검증하기 위한 추가 연구가 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research reveals that doubt and uncertainty are not merely a lack of information but rather an unpleasant emotional state. Psychologists discovered that the negative feeling we experience when thinking deeply about difficult problems is actually an important signal mechanism. The research team presented participants with cognitive tasks of varying difficulty and measured their emotional responses. Results showed that when people abandoned intuitive thinking (mental shortcuts) and shifted to critical thinking, they experienced discomfort—and this emotion drove more careful and accurate judgments. The findings suggest that doubt and anxiety are not obstacles but evolutionarily useful signaling systems. However, the study was conducted in controlled laboratory settings, so additional research is needed to verify its applicability in real-world, everyday situations.

##
keyPoints: **의심은 감정이다** / Doubt is an emotional state, not just information gap
**부정적 감정이 좋은 역할을 한다** / Negative emotions serve as necessary alarm bells for critical thinking
**직관 포기의 신호** / The discomfort signals abandoning mental shortcuts and engaging deeper cognition
**진화적 유용성** / Doubt represents an evolutionarily adaptive mechanism for better decision-making

articles.title: Doing this throughout life may cut Alzheimer’s risk by 38%
articles.titleKo: 평생 이를 실천하면 알츠하이머병 위험을 38% 감소시킬 수 있습니다
articles.summary: A lifetime of mental stimulation—like reading, writing, and learning new skills—may help protect the brain as we age. People with the highest levels of cognitive enrichment had a much lower risk of Alzheimer’s and experienced symptoms years later than those with the lowest levels.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated whether lifelong mental stimulation—such as reading, writing, and acquiring new skills—can reduce the risk of Alzheimer's disease. Researchers measured participants' levels of cognitive enrichment and tracked their outcomes over time. Results showed that individuals with the highest levels of cognitive stimulation experienced a 38% reduction in Alzheimer's risk compared to those with the lowest levels, and delayed symptom onset by several years. These findings suggest that strengthening cognitive reserve through mental engagement may provide protection against neurodegenerative diseases. However, as an observational study, it does not establish causation, and potential confounding variables such as socioeconomic status and educational background could not be entirely controlled for.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 평생에 걸친 정신적 자극이 알츠하이머병 발병 위험을 줄일 수 있다는 가설을 검증했습니다. 연구진은 독서, 글쓰기, 새로운 기술 학습 등의 인지적 풍요로움(cognitive enrichment) 수준을 측정하여 참가자들을 추적 조사했습니다. 결과적으로 인지적 자극이 가장 높은 수준의 사람들은 가장 낮은 수준의 사람들 대비 알츠하이머병 위험을 약 38% 감소시켰으며, 증상 발현 시기를 수 년 더 늦출 수 있었습니다. 이는 뇌의 인지 예비력(cognitive reserve)을 강화하면 신경퇴행성 질환으로부터 보호할 수 있음을 시사합니다. 다만 본 연구는 관찰 연구로 인과관계를 명확히 증명하지는 못했으며, 사회경제적 지위나 교육 수준 등 혼재변수의 영향을 완전히 배제할 수 없다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigated whether lifelong mental stimulation—such as reading, writing, and acquiring new skills—can reduce the risk of Alzheimer's disease. Researchers measured participants' levels of cognitive enrichment and tracked their outcomes over time. Results showed that individuals with the highest levels of cognitive stimulation experienced a 38% reduction in Alzheimer's risk compared to those with the lowest levels, and delayed symptom onset by several years. These findings suggest that strengthening cognitive reserve through mental engagement may provide protection against neurodegenerative diseases. However, as an observational study, it does not establish causation, and potential confounding variables such as socioeconomic status and educational background could not be entirely controlled for.

##
keyPoints: **38% 위험 감소** / 38% risk reduction: 높은 수준의 인지적 자극은 알츠하이머병 발병 위험을 약 38% 감소시킴
**증상 발현 지연** / Delayed symptom onset: 정신 활동이 많은 사람들이 알츠하이머 증상을 수 년 더 늦게 경험하는 경향
**인지 예비력 강화** / Cognitive reserve enhancement: 독서, 글쓰기, 새로운 기술 학습 등이 뇌의 보호 메커니즘을 구축
**생애 전반의 개입** / Lifelong intervention: 특정 시기가 아닌 평생에 걸친 지속적인 정신적 자극이 중요하다는 점

articles.title: Scientists say we’ve been treating Alzheimer’s all wrong
articles.titleKo: 과학자들 "알츠하이머병 치료, 우리가 완전히 잘못 접근했다"
articles.summary: Alzheimer’s isn’t just one problem—it’s a tangled mix of biology, aging, and overall health. That’s why drugs targeting a single factor have fallen short, even as new treatments show modest benefits. Scientists are now pushing toward multi-pronged strategies, from gene editing to brain-cell rejuvenation and gut health interventions. The goal: stop treating Alzheimer’s as one disease and start tackling it as a complex system.
articles.aiSummary:
Alzheimer's disease is increasingly recognized by the scientific community not as a simple brain condition but as a complex disorder involving intertwined biological factors, aging processes, and overall health status. Traditional pharmaceutical approaches have focused on targeting single factors, yet this strategy has proven to have limited effectiveness in treating the disease. Although recent medications show modest therapeutic benefits, scientists are advocating for integrated, multi-pronged treatment strategies including gene editing, brain-cell rejuvenation, and gut microbiome interventions. The significance of this paradigm shift lies in reconceptualizing Alzheimer's not as a single disease entity but as a complex biological system requiring systemic intervention. However, this multifaceted approach faces limitations, including the need for further clarification of interaction mechanisms between different treatments and extensive long-term clinical trials before practical clinical implementation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
알츠하이머병은 단순한 뇌 질환이 아니라 생물학적 요인, 노화, 전반적인 건강 상태가 복잡하게 얽혀 있는 질환이라는 새로운 과학적 인식이 확산되고 있다. 그동안 의약학 계는 특정 단일 요인만을 겨냥한 약물 개발에 집중했으나, 이러한 접근법의 효과가 제한적임이 밝혀졌다. 최근의 신약들이 어느 정도의 긍정적 효과를 보이고 있지만, 과학자들은 유전자 편집, 뇌세포 재생, 장내 미생물 관리 등 여러 방향의 통합적 전략으로 전환할 것을 주장하고 있다. 이러한 연구 의의는 알츠하이머병을 단일 질환으로 보지 않고 복잡한 생물학적 체계로 이해하려는 패러다임 전환에 있다. 다만 이러한 다층적 접근이 실제 임상에 적용되기까지는 각 치료법의 상호작용 메커니즘 규명과 장기적 임상시험이 필요한 한계를 지니고 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Alzheimer's disease is increasingly recognized by the scientific community not as a simple brain condition but as a complex disorder involving intertwined biological factors, aging processes, and overall health status. Traditional pharmaceutical approaches have focused on targeting single factors, yet this strategy has proven to have limited effectiveness in treating the disease. Although recent medications show modest therapeutic benefits, scientists are advocating for integrated, multi-pronged treatment strategies including gene editing, brain-cell rejuvenation, and gut microbiome interventions. The significance of this paradigm shift lies in reconceptualizing Alzheimer's not as a single disease entity but as a complex biological system requiring systemic intervention. However, this multifaceted approach faces limitations, including the need for further clarification of interaction mechanisms between different treatments and extensive long-term clinical trials before practical clinical implementation.

##
keyPoints: 단일 요인 치료의 한계 / Limitations of single-factor treatment approaches, necessitating a shift toward more comprehensive strategies
복합적 원인 규명 / Recognition that Alzheimer's involves complex interactions between biology, aging, and overall health status
다층적 치료 전략 필요 / Multi-pronged treatment strategies including gene therapy, cellular rejuvenation, and microbiome interventions
질환 개념의 재정의 / Paradigm shift from viewing Alzheimer's as a single disease to understanding it as a complex biological system requiring systemic intervention

articles.title: The Non-Optimality of Scientific Knowledge: Path Dependence, Lock-In, and The Local Minimum Trap
articles.titleKo: 과학적 지식의 비최적성: 경로 의존성, 잠금(Lock-In), 그리고 국소 최소값의 함정
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11828v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Science is widely regarded as humanity's most reliable method for uncovering truths about the natural world. Yet the \emph{trajectory} of scientific discovery is rarely examined as an optimization problem in its own right. This paper argues that the body of scientific knowledge, at any given historical moment, represents a \emph{local optimum} rather than a global one--that the frameworks, formalisms, and paradigms through which we understand nature are substantially shaped by historical contingency, cognitive path dependence, and institutional l
articles.aiSummary:
This research challenges the common assumption that science represents the optimal path to understanding nature, arguing instead that scientific knowledge at any historical moment constitutes a *local optimum* rather than a *global optimum*. The study posits that the frameworks, formalisms, and paradigms through which we comprehend nature are substantially shaped by historical contingency, cognitive path dependence, and institutional factors rather than pure merit-based discovery. This implies that scientific progress is constrained by prior choices and decisions, and under different initial conditions, entirely different knowledge systems could have developed. The research fundamentally questions conventional assumptions about scientific objectivity and contributes to our understanding of the limitations inherent in scientific methodology. However, the analysis remains primarily theoretical without presenting specific historical case studies or empirical data to substantiate the arguments, which represents a notable limitation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 과학이 자연의 진실을 밝히는 가장 신뢰할 수 있는 방법으로 간주되지만, 과학적 발견의 궤적이 최적화 문제로서 거의 검토되지 않는다는 점에 주목합니다. 연구진은 특정 역사적 시점의 과학 지식이 전역 최적값(최고의 상태)이 아닌 국소 최적값(국한된 범위에서의 최고 상태)을 나타낸다고 주장합니다. 이는 자연을 이해하는 틀, 형식, 패러다임이 역사적 우연성, 인지적 경로 의존성, 그리고 제도적 요인에 의해 크게 영향받는다는 의미입니다. 연구는 과학적 진보가 이전의 선택과 결정에 의해 제약받으며, 다른 초기 조건이었다면 완전히 다른 지식 체계가 발전했을 가능성을 제시합니다. 이 분석은 과학의 객관성에 대한 일반적인 가정에 도전하며, 과학적 방법론의 한계를 이해하는 데 중요합니다. 다만 구체적인 역사적 사례나 실증 데이터가 제시되지 않아 이론적 논의 수준에 머물러 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research challenges the common assumption that science represents the optimal path to understanding nature, arguing instead that scientific knowledge at any historical moment constitutes a *local optimum* rather than a *global optimum*. The study posits that the frameworks, formalisms, and paradigms through which we comprehend nature are substantially shaped by historical contingency, cognitive path dependence, and institutional factors rather than pure merit-based discovery. This implies that scientific progress is constrained by prior choices and decisions, and under different initial conditions, entirely different knowledge systems could have developed. The research fundamentally questions conventional assumptions about scientific objectivity and contributes to our understanding of the limitations inherent in scientific methodology. However, the analysis remains primarily theoretical without presenting specific historical case studies or empirical data to substantiate the arguments, which represents a notable limitation.

##
keyPoints: **국소 최적값의 함정** / Local Optimum Trap: 과학적 지식은 절대적 진실이 아닌 특정 역사적 맥락 내에서의 최선의 상태에 불과하며, 더 나은 대안이 존재할 수 있음 / Scientific knowledge represents the best solution within specific historical contexts, not absolute truth, and superior alternatives may exist.

**경로 의존성(Path Dependence)** / Historical Contingency: 초기의 과학적 선택과 패러다임이 이후 발전 방향을 결정하며, 다른 초기 조건이었다면 완전히 다른 과학 체계가 형성되었을 수 있음 / Early scientific choices and paradigms determine subsequent development directions, and different initial conditions could have led to entirely different scientific systems.

**제도적 제약** / Institutional Lock-In: 대학, 학술지, 학파 등 과학 제도가 기존 패러다임을 강화하고 혁신적 대안의 수용을 어렵게 함 / Scientific institutions like universities and academic journals reinforce existing paradigms and inhibit acceptance of radical alternatives.

**객관성에 대한 재검토** / Objectivity Reconsidered: 과학의 객관성은 순수한 증거 기반이 아니라 역사적 맥락, 인지적 편향, 제도적 이익에 의해 중대하게 영향받음 / Scientific objectivity is substantially influenced by historical context, cognitive biases, and institutional interests, not purely by evidence alone.

articles.title: Self-Monitoring Benefits from Structural Integration: Lessons from Metacognition in Continuous-Time Multi-Timescale Agents
articles.titleKo: 연속시간 다중시간척도 에이전트의 메타인지로부터의 교훈: 구조적 통합에서의 자기-모니터링 이점
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11914v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-monitoring capabilities -- metacognition, self-prediction, and subjective duration -- are often proposed as useful additions to reinforcement learning agents. But do they actually help? We investigate this question in a continuous-time multi-timescale agent operating in predator-prey survival environments of varying complexity, including a 2D partially observable variant. We first show that three self-monitoring modules, implemented as auxiliary-loss add-ons to a multi-timescale cortical hierarchy, provide no statistically significant benefi
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates whether self-monitoring capabilities—metacognition, self-prediction, and subjective time perception—actually benefit reinforcement learning agents. The research team conducted experiments using a continuous-time multi-timescale agent operating in predator-prey survival environments of varying complexity, including a 2D partially observable environment. Initial results showed that three self-monitoring modules, implemented as auxiliary-loss add-ons, did not provide statistically significant performance improvements when used independently. However, when these modules were structurally integrated into the neural network, the agent's performance improved meaningfully. The study suggests that self-monitoring functions are more effective when properly integrated into the architecture rather than simply added as auxiliary components. Further validation of generalizability across different environments is needed.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 강화학습 에이전트에 자기 모니터링 능력(메타인지, 자기예측, 주관적 시간 인지)이 실제로 도움이 되는지를 검증하는 것을 목표로 합니다. 연구팀은 포식자-피식자 생존 환경에서 작동하는 다중 시간 척도 연동 에이전트를 사용하여 실험을 설계했으며, 2차원 부분 관찰 가능 환경을 포함한 다양한 복잡도의 환경을 구성했습니다. 초기 결과로 세 가지 자기 모니터링 모듈(보조 손실 추가로 구현)이 단독으로는 통계적으로 유의미한 성능 향상을 제공하지 못했습니다. 그러나 이러한 모듈들을 구조적으로 통합했을 때, 에이전트의 성능이 의미 있게 개선되었습니다. 본 연구는 자기 모니터링 기능이 단순히 추가되는 것보다 신경망의 구조 내에 적절히 통합될 때 그 효과를 발휘할 수 있음을 시사합니다. 다만, 특정 환경에서의 일반화 가능성 검증이 추가로 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigates whether self-monitoring capabilities—metacognition, self-prediction, and subjective time perception—actually benefit reinforcement learning agents. The research team conducted experiments using a continuous-time multi-timescale agent operating in predator-prey survival environments of varying complexity, including a 2D partially observable environment. Initial results showed that three self-monitoring modules, implemented as auxiliary-loss add-ons, did not provide statistically significant performance improvements when used independently. However, when these modules were structurally integrated into the neural network, the agent's performance improved meaningfully. The study suggests that self-monitoring functions are more effective when properly integrated into the architecture rather than simply added as auxiliary components. Further validation of generalizability across different environments is needed.

##
keyPoints: **자기 모니터링의 역할 재정의** / Redefining the Role of Self-Monitoring: 메타인지와 같은 심리학 개념이 AI 에이전트에도 필요하다는 가정을 실증적으로 검증하는 연구
**구조적 통합의 중요성** / Importance of Structural Integration: 보조 손실(auxiliary loss)로만 추가하는 것보다 신경망의 기본 구조에 통합할 때 성능 향상 효과가 나타남
**다중 시간 척도 처리** / Multi-Timescale Processing: 연속 시간 환경에서 서로 다른 속도의 의사결정을 처리할 수 있는 에이전트 설계의 효율성
**실험 환경의 확대 필요** / Need for Expanded Experimental Validation: 포식자-피식자 환경에서의 결과가 다른 복잡한 환경으로 얼마나 일반화되는지 추가 검증 필요

articles.title: GoodPoint: Learning Constructive Scientific Paper Feedback from Author Responses
articles.titleKo: 좋은 피드백: 저자 응답으로부터 건설적인 과학 논문 피드백 학습하기
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While LLMs hold significant potential to transform scientific research, we advocate for their use to augment and empower researchers rather than to automate research without human oversight. To this end, we study constructive feedback generation, the task of producing targeted, actionable feedback that helps authors improve both their research and its presentation. In this work, we operationalize the effectiveness of feedback along two author-centric axes-validity and author action. We first curate GoodPoint-ICLR, a dataset of 19K ICLR papers wit
articles.aiSummary:
This research advocates for using large language models (LLMs) to augment researchers rather than automate scientific research without oversight. The study focuses on generating "constructive feedback"—targeted, actionable comments that help authors improve both their research and its presentation. The researchers defined feedback effectiveness along two author-centric dimensions: validity and author action (whether authors actually implement the feedback). They created the GoodPoint-ICLR dataset comprising 19,000 ICLR conference papers paired with author responses, enabling LLMs to learn which types of feedback actually lead to paper revisions. The significance of this work lies in demonstrating that AI can be repositioned from a evaluative tool to a developmental tool that supports researcher growth. However, the study's generalizability is limited as it focuses exclusively on ICLR papers in computer science, requiring further validation across different academic disciplines and research cultures.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대규모 언어모델(LLM)이 과학 연구를 자동화하기보다는 연구자를 돕는 도구로 사용되어야 한다는 관점에서 출발했습니다. 연구팀은 논문 저자들이 실제로 개선할 수 있는 '건설적인 피드백' 생성에 초점을 맞추었습니다. 효과적인 피드백의 기준을 '타당성(validity)'과 '저자 행동(author action)' 두 가지로 정의하고, ICLR 학술대회의 19,000개 논문과 저자 응답 데이터로 'GoodPoint-ICLR' 데이터셋을 구축했습니다. 이 데이터셋을 통해 LLM이 어떤 피드백이 실제로 저자들의 논문 수정으로 이어지는지 학습하도록 훈련시켰습니다. 연구의 주요 의의는 AI가 단순히 평가하는 것이 아니라 연구자의 성장을 돕는 방향으로 사용될 수 있음을 보여줬다는 점입니다. 다만 연구는 ICLR이라는 특정 분야의 논문에만 기반했으므로, 다른 학문 분야나 연구 문화에 적용 가능성은 추가 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research advocates for using large language models (LLMs) to augment researchers rather than automate scientific research without oversight. The study focuses on generating "constructive feedback"—targeted, actionable comments that help authors improve both their research and its presentation. The researchers defined feedback effectiveness along two author-centric dimensions: validity and author action (whether authors actually implement the feedback). They created the GoodPoint-ICLR dataset comprising 19,000 ICLR conference papers paired with author responses, enabling LLMs to learn which types of feedback actually lead to paper revisions. The significance of this work lies in demonstrating that AI can be repositioned from a evaluative tool to a developmental tool that supports researcher growth. However, the study's generalizability is limited as it focuses exclusively on ICLR papers in computer science, requiring further validation across different academic disciplines and research cultures.

##
keyPoints: **LLM의 역할 재정의** / Repositioning LLM Role: 인공지능을 연구 자동화 도구에서 연구자 지원 도구로 전환하는 새로운 접근 방식 제시 (Shift from automation to augmentation in scientific research)

**저자 응답 기반 학습** / Learning from Author Responses: 19,000개 논문과 실제 저자 피드백 데이터를 활용하여 '실제 효과 있는 피드백'의 특성을 학습 (Training on authentic author responses to understand what feedback drives actual improvements)

**이중 평가 기준** / Dual-Axis Effectiveness Metric: 피드백의 타당성(validity)과 저자의 실제 행동(author action) 두 가지 지표로 피드백 효과성을 측정 (Measuring feedback effectiveness through both theoretical validity and practical implementation)

**학제 간 적용 필요성** / Cross-Disciplinary Application Gap: 현재 컴퓨터 과학 분야(ICLR)에만 국한되어 있어, 다른 학문 분야의 연구 문화와 피드백 선호도 반영 필요 (Limited to one discipline; generalizability across different fields remains to be tested)

articles.title: Narrative-Driven Paper-to-Slide Generation via ArcDeck
articles.titleKo: ArcDeck을 활용한 서사 기반 논문-슬라이드 자동 생성
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11969v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce ArcDeck, a multi-agent framework that formulates paper-to-slide generation as a structured narrative reconstruction task. Unlike existing methods that directly summarize raw text into slides, ArcDeck explicitly models the source paper's logical flow. It first parses the input to construct a discourse tree and establish a global commitment document, ensuring the high-level intent is preserved. These structural priors then guide an iterative multi-agent refinement process, where specialized agents iteratively critique and revise the pr
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the automatic conversion of academic papers into presentation slides using a novel approach called ArcDeck. While existing methods simply summarize paper text into slides, this research proposes a structured method that explicitly preserves the logical flow of source papers. The researchers developed a multi-agent framework that first constructs a discourse tree to analyze the paper's structure and creates a global commitment document to capture the paper's high-level intent. Based on these structural foundations, multiple specialized AI agents iteratively review and refine the generated slides through a collaborative refinement process. This approach more effectively reflects the key content and logical connections of the paper in the final slides. However, as this is a preprint stage on arXiv, large-scale experimental results and quantitative evaluation metrics may be limited.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 학술논문을 프레젠테이션 슬라이드로 자동 변환하는 문제를 다루고 있습니다. 기존 방법들이 논문의 텍스트를 단순히 요약하는 방식을 사용했다면, 이 연구에서 제안하는 ArcDeck은 논문의 논리적 흐름을 체계적으로 분석하고 보존하는 새로운 접근 방식을 제시합니다. 연구팀은 다중 에이전트 프레임워크를 개발하여 먼저 논문의 구조를 분석하는 담론 트리(discourse tree)를 구축하고, 논문의 핵심 의도를 정리한 글로벌 약속 문서(commitment document)를 작성합니다. 이러한 구조적 정보를 바탕으로, 여러 개의 전문화된 AI 에이전트들이 반복적으로 슬라이드를 검토하고 수정하는 과정을 거칩니다. 이 방법은 논문의 주요 내용과 논리적 연결성을 더욱 효과적으로 슬라이드에 반영할 수 있게 합니다. 다만, 연구가 arXiv 프레프린트 단계이므로 대규모 실험 결과와 정량적 평가 데이터가 제한적일 수 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study addresses the automatic conversion of academic papers into presentation slides using a novel approach called ArcDeck. While existing methods simply summarize paper text into slides, this research proposes a structured method that explicitly preserves the logical flow of source papers. The researchers developed a multi-agent framework that first constructs a discourse tree to analyze the paper's structure and creates a global commitment document to capture the paper's high-level intent. Based on these structural foundations, multiple specialized AI agents iteratively review and refine the generated slides through a collaborative refinement process. This approach more effectively reflects the key content and logical connections of the paper in the final slides. However, as this is a preprint stage on arXiv, large-scale experimental results and quantitative evaluation metrics may be limited.

##
keyPoints: **포인트1** / Point 1: 기존의 단순 텍스트 요약 방식에서 벗어나 논문의 논리적 구조를 명시적으로 모델링하는 혁신적 접근 / Revolutionary approach that explicitly models paper's logical structure instead of simple text summarization
**포인트2** / Point 2: 담론 트리와 글로벌 약속 문서라는 구조적 사전정보를 활용하여 논문의 고차적 의도 보존 / Preservation of high-level intent through discourse trees and commitment documents as structural priors
**포인트3** / Point 3: 다중 에이전트 프레임워크를 통한 반복적 검토·수정 프로세스로 슬라이드 품질 향상 / Iterative multi-agent refinement process where specialized agents critique and revise slides for improved quality
**포인트4** / Point 4: 자동화된 논문-슬라이드 변환으로 학술 발표 준비 시간 단축 및 논리적 일관성 강화 가능 / Automated paper-to-slide conversion reduces presentation preparation time while enhancing logical consistency

articles.title: Uncertainty Quantification in CNN Through the Bootstrap of Convex Neural Networks
articles.titleKo: CNN에서 볼록 신경망 부트스트랩을 통한 불확실성 정량화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11833v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the popularity of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), the problem of uncertainty quantification (UQ) of CNN has been largely overlooked. Lack of efficient UQ tools severely limits the application of CNN in certain areas, such as medicine, where prediction uncertainty is critically important. Among the few existing UQ approaches that have been proposed for deep learning, none of them has theoretical consistency that can guarantee the uncertainty quality. To address this issue, we propose a novel bootstrap based framework for the estimatio
articles.aiSummary:
Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) perform exceptionally well in image recognition but lack efficient uncertainty quantification (UQ) tools, severely limiting their application in critical fields like medicine where prediction reliability is essential. This study proposes a novel bootstrap-based framework leveraging convex neural networks to address this limitation and provide theoretically consistent uncertainty quantification for CNNs. Unlike existing deep learning UQ approaches, this method guarantees uncertainty quality through theoretical consistency. The specific performance metrics will be validated through peer-reviewed academic evaluation, potentially enabling broader application of AI in safety-critical domains such as medical diagnosis and autonomous driving. However, the research is in its early developmental stage, and further validation is needed using large-scale medical datasets and diverse industrial environments to test its practical effectiveness and robustness.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
합성곱 신경망(CNN)은 이미지 인식 등에서 뛰어난 성능을 보이지만, 예측 결과가 얼마나 신뢰할 수 있는지 측정하는 '불확실성 정량화' 기술이 부족하여 의료 진단처럼 신뢰성이 중요한 분야에 적용하기 어렵다는 문제가 있습니다. 본 연구는 이러한 한계를 극복하기 위해 볼록 신경망(convex neural network)의 부트스트랩 기법을 활용한 새로운 불확실성 정량화 프레임워크를 제안합니다. 이 방법은 기존의 딥러닝 불확실성 정량화 접근법과 달리 이론적 일관성을 갖춰 불확실성 품질을 보장할 수 있다는 장점이 있습니다. 구체적인 성능 수치는 전문 학술지 평가에서 확인할 수 있으며, 이 연구는 의료·자율주행 등 안전성이 중요한 AI 응용 분야를 확대할 수 있는 기초를 마련했습니다. 다만 현재는 기술 개발 초기 단계로, 실제 대규모 의료 데이터나 다양한 산업 환경에서의 검증이 필요한 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) perform exceptionally well in image recognition but lack efficient uncertainty quantification (UQ) tools, severely limiting their application in critical fields like medicine where prediction reliability is essential. This study proposes a novel bootstrap-based framework leveraging convex neural networks to address this limitation and provide theoretically consistent uncertainty quantification for CNNs. Unlike existing deep learning UQ approaches, this method guarantees uncertainty quality through theoretical consistency. The specific performance metrics will be validated through peer-reviewed academic evaluation, potentially enabling broader application of AI in safety-critical domains such as medical diagnosis and autonomous driving. However, the research is in its early developmental stage, and further validation is needed using large-scale medical datasets and diverse industrial environments to test its practical effectiveness and robustness.

##
keyPoints: **CNN의 불확실성 정량화 부족 문제 / Lack of Uncertainty Quantification in CNNs**: 기존 CNN은 예측 결과의 신뢰도를 정량화하지 못해 의료 등 고위험 분야 적용이 제한됨 / Existing CNNs fail to quantify prediction reliability, limiting their application in high-risk domains like healthcare

**이론적 일관성을 갖춘 부트스트랩 기반 솔루션 / Theoretically Consistent Bootstrap Framework**: 볼록 신경망을 활용한 새로운 UQ 프레임워크로 기존 방법의 이론적 결함 해결 / Novel UQ framework using convex neural networks addresses theoretical gaps in existing approaches

**의료·자율주행 등 안전성 중심 응용 확대 / Enabling Safety-Critical Applications**: 신뢰할 수 있는 불확실성 정량화를 통해 AI의 적용 범위를 의료 진단, 자율주행 등으로 확대 가능 / Reliable UQ enables broader AI application in medical diagnosis, autonomous driving, and other critical sectors

**추가 검증 필요 / Need for Further Validation**: 아직 초기 개발 단계로 실제 대규모 데이터셋과 다양한 산업 환경에서의 실증적 검증 필요 / Early-stage research requiring empirical validation on large-scale datasets and diverse industry environments

articles.title: Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning with LLMs for Generalizable Multimodal Clinical Reasoning
articles.titleKo: LLM을 이용한 스키마 적응형 표형 데이터 표현 학습과 일반화 가능한 다중양식 임상 추론
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning for tabular data remains constrained by poor schema generalization, a challenge rooted in the lack of semantic understanding of structured variables. This challenge is particularly acute in domains like clinical medicine, where electronic health record (EHR) schemas vary significantly. To solve this problem, we propose Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning, a novel method that leverages large language models (LLMs) to create transferable tabular embeddings. By transforming structured variables into semantic natural lang
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the schema generalization challenge in clinical machine learning, where electronic health record (EHR) systems vary significantly across different hospitals, limiting AI model transferability. The research team proposes Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning, a novel method leveraging large language models to convert structured variables into semantic natural language, creating transferable tabular embeddings. By transforming different data formats into meaningful text representations, the method enables medical AI systems to better understand diverse data structures across institutions. This approach aims to improve compatibility between different hospital EHR systems and enhance model generalization performance in multimodal clinical reasoning tasks. The research has significant implications for improving clinical AI accuracy and promoting real-world hospital implementation. However, the study's limitations include computational costs of large language models and the need for validation across diverse clinical environments.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 임상의학 분야에서 전자의무기록(EHR) 데이터의 스키마(데이터 구조) 차이로 인한 AI 모델의 일반화 문제를 해결하고자 시작되었다. 연구팀은 대규모 언어모델(LLM)을 활용하여 표 형식의 의료 데이터를 의미 있는 텍스트로 변환한 후, 이를 통해 전이 가능한 임베딩(수치 표현)을 생성하는 새로운 방법을 개발했다. 이 Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning 방법은 서로 다른 병원의 EHR 시스템 간 호환성을 높이고, 모델의 일반화 성능을 향상시키는 것을 목표로 한다. 구조화된 변수들을 자연언어로 변환함으로써 의료 AI 시스템이 다양한 데이터 형식을 더 잘 이해할 수 있게 한다. 이는 임상 추론의 정확성 향상과 실제 병원 환경에서의 AI 활용을 촉진할 수 있는 의의가 있다. 다만, 대규모 언어모델의 계산 비용과 다양한 임상 환경에서의 검증이 추가로 필요하다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses the schema generalization challenge in clinical machine learning, where electronic health record (EHR) systems vary significantly across different hospitals, limiting AI model transferability. The research team proposes Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning, a novel method leveraging large language models to convert structured variables into semantic natural language, creating transferable tabular embeddings. By transforming different data formats into meaningful text representations, the method enables medical AI systems to better understand diverse data structures across institutions. This approach aims to improve compatibility between different hospital EHR systems and enhance model generalization performance in multimodal clinical reasoning tasks. The research has significant implications for improving clinical AI accuracy and promoting real-world hospital implementation. However, the study's limitations include computational costs of large language models and the need for validation across diverse clinical environments.

##
keyPoints: **문제정의**: 병원마다 다른 전자의무기록(EHR) 데이터 구조로 인해 기계학습 모델이 새로운 환경에 적응하지 못하는 스키마 일반화 문제 / Problem: Poor schema generalization due to varying EHR structures across hospitals, limiting AI model transferability

**혁신적 해결책**: 대규모 언어모델(LLM)을 이용해 표 형식 데이터를 의미 있는 자연언어로 변환하여 의료 데이터의 의미를 기계에 이해시키는 방법 / Solution: Using LLMs to transform structured tabular data into semantic natural language, enabling machines to understand clinical data meaning

**실제 응용 가치**: 다양한 병원 간 의료 AI 시스템의 호환성 증대 및 임상 추론 정확성 향상으로 실제 진료 현장에서의 AI 활용 확대 / Clinical Impact: Enhanced compatibility between different hospital systems and improved clinical reasoning accuracy for real-world medical implementation

**기술적 혁신**: 단순한 데이터 변환을 넘어 멀티모달(텍스트+숫자) 임상 추론이 가능한 전이 가능한 임베딩(수치 표현) 생성 / Technical Advancement: Creating transferable multimodal embeddings that enable clinical reasoning across different data formats and hospital systems

articles.title: A Layer-wise Analysis of Supervised Fine-Tuning
articles.titleKo: 지도 학습 미세 조정의 계층별 분석
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While critical for alignment, Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) incurs the risk of catastrophic forgetting, yet the layer-wise emergence of instruction-following capabilities remains elusive. We investigate this mechanism via a comprehensive analysis utilizing information-theoretic, geometric, and optimization metrics across model scales (1B-32B). Our experiments reveal a distinct depth-dependent pattern: middle layers (20\%-80\%) are stable, whereas final layers exhibit high sensitivity. Leveraging this insight, we propose Mid-Block Efficient Tuning,
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates catastrophic forgetting in Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), where language models learn to follow human instructions but may lose previously acquired knowledge. The team conducted a comprehensive layer-wise analysis across four model scales (1B to 32B parameters) using information-theoretic, geometric, and optimization metrics. The key finding reveals a depth-dependent pattern: middle layers (occupying 20%-80% of model depth) remain stable during fine-tuning, while final layers exhibit high sensitivity and undergo significant changes. Based on this insight, the researchers propose Mid-Block Efficient Tuning, a novel approach that optimizes training efficiency. The study provides important mechanistic understanding of how instruction-following capabilities emerge in neural networks, though further validation on real-world applications would strengthen the findings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대규모 언어모델을 인간의 지시에 따르도록 학습시키는 '지도학습 미세조정(SFT)' 과정에서 이전 지식이 손실되는 문제를 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 1억 개에서 320억 개의 파라미터를 가진 다양한 크기의 모델 4개를 대상으로, 정보이론·기하학·최적화 지표를 활용하여 층별 분석을 수행했습니다. 핵심 발견은 신경망의 깊이에 따라 학습 패턴이 다르다는 것으로, 중간층(전체 깊이의 20%-80%)은 안정적이지만 최종층은 매우 민감하게 반응한다는 점입니다. 이를 바탕으로 연구팀은 '중간 블록 효율적 조정(Mid-Block Efficient Tuning)'이라는 새로운 방법을 제안했습니다. 이 연구는 언어모델이 어떻게 명령을 학습하는지의 메커니즘을 이해하는 데 중요하지만, 추가적인 실제 적용 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigates catastrophic forgetting in Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), where language models learn to follow human instructions but may lose previously acquired knowledge. The team conducted a comprehensive layer-wise analysis across four model scales (1B to 32B parameters) using information-theoretic, geometric, and optimization metrics. The key finding reveals a depth-dependent pattern: middle layers (occupying 20%-80% of model depth) remain stable during fine-tuning, while final layers exhibit high sensitivity and undergo significant changes. Based on this insight, the researchers propose Mid-Block Efficient Tuning, a novel approach that optimizes training efficiency. The study provides important mechanistic understanding of how instruction-following capabilities emerge in neural networks, though further validation on real-world applications would strengthen the findings.

##
keyPoints: **층별 안정성의 차이 발견** / Discovery of layer-dependent stability differences: 신경망의 중간층은 미세조정 중 안정적으로 유지되지만 최종층은 민감하게 변한다는 흥미로운 패턴 발견
**정보이론적·기하학적 분석** / Multi-faceted analytical approach: 정보이론, 기하학, 최적화 등 다양한 분석 방법을 활용하여 포괄적 이해 제시
**4가지 모델 규모 검증** / Validation across model scales: 1B에서 32B 파라미터까지 다양한 크기의 모델에서 일관된 패턴 확인
**Mid-Block Efficient Tuning 제안** / Practical optimization solution: 발견된 패턴을 기반으로 효율적인 미세조정 방법 제시하여 응용 가능성 제공

articles.title: When Reasoning Models Hurt Behavioral Simulation: A Solver-Sampler Mismatch in Multi-Agent LLM Negotiation
articles.titleKo: 추론 모델이 행동 시뮬레이션을 방해할 때: 다중 에이전트 LLM 협상에서의 풀이자-샘플러 불일치
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.11840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used as agents in social, economic, and policy simulations. A common assumption is that stronger reasoning should improve simulation fidelity. We argue that this assumption can fail when the objective is not to solve a strategic problem, but to sample plausible boundedly rational behavior. In such settings, reasoning-enhanced models can become better solvers and worse simulators: they can over-optimize for strategically dominant actions, collapse compromise-oriented terminal behavior, and sometimes exhibit a
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses a paradoxical problem that arises when using large language models (LLMs) as agents in social, economic, and policy simulations. While stronger reasoning capabilities are typically assumed to improve simulation fidelity, the research reveals this assumption can fail in certain contexts. The team discovered that reasoning-enhanced LLMs fail to accurately simulate boundedly rational human behavior in multi-agent negotiation tasks. Specifically, stronger reasoning models over-optimize for strategically dominant actions, collapse compromise-oriented behaviors, and sometimes exhibit unpredictable patterns. The research introduces the concept of "Solver-Sampler Mismatch," demonstrating that increased model reasoning capability can paradoxically degrade performance in realistic human behavior simulation. This finding fundamentally challenges the reliability of LLM-based simulations and calls for reconsidering model selection criteria for behavioral simulation purposes, distinguishing between strategic problem-solving and behavioral sampling objectives.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대규모 언어모델(LLM)을 사회·경제·정책 시뮬레이션의 에이전트로 사용할 때 발생하는 역설적 문제를 다룬다. 일반적으로 더 강력한 추론 능력이 시뮬레이션의 정확성을 높일 것으로 예상되지만, 실제로는 그 반대의 결과가 나타날 수 있다는 것이 핵심이다. 연구팀은 다중 에이전트 협상 과제에서 추론 능력이 강한 LLM들이 현실적인 제한된 합리성을 가진 인간 행동을 모사하는 데 실패한다는 것을 발견했다. 구체적으로, 추론이 강화된 모델들은 전략적으로 지배적인 행동으로 과도하게 최적화되어 타협 지향적 행동을 붕괴시키고 때로는 예측 불가능한 행동을 보였다. 이는 "해결자-샘플러 불일치(Solver-Sampler Mismatch)"라는 새로운 개념을 제시하며, 모델의 추론 능력이 강할수록 현실적인 인간 행동 시뮬레이션에서 오히려 성능이 저하될 수 있음을 시사한다. 이 연구의 의의는 LLM 기반 시뮬레이션의 신뢰성에 대한 근본적인 질문을 제기하며, 향후 행동 시뮬레이션을 위한 모델 선택 기준을 재고하도록 촉구한다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study addresses a paradoxical problem that arises when using large language models (LLMs) as agents in social, economic, and policy simulations. While stronger reasoning capabilities are typically assumed to improve simulation fidelity, the research reveals this assumption can fail in certain contexts. The team discovered that reasoning-enhanced LLMs fail to accurately simulate boundedly rational human behavior in multi-agent negotiation tasks. Specifically, stronger reasoning models over-optimize for strategically dominant actions, collapse compromise-oriented behaviors, and sometimes exhibit unpredictable patterns. The research introduces the concept of "Solver-Sampler Mismatch," demonstrating that increased model reasoning capability can paradoxically degrade performance in realistic human behavior simulation. This finding fundamentally challenges the reliability of LLM-based simulations and calls for reconsidering model selection criteria for behavioral simulation purposes, distinguishing between strategic problem-solving and behavioral sampling objectives.

##
keyPoints: **추론 강화의 역설** / Reasoning Paradox: 더 강력한 추론 능력을 가진 LLM이 현실적인 인간 행동 모사에서 오히려 성능 저하
**해결자-샘플러 불일치** / Solver-Sampler Mismatch: 전략적 문제 해결과 현실적 행동 샘플링이라는 서로 다른 목표 간의 본질적 충돌
**협상 행동의 붕괴** / Collapse of Negotiation Behavior: 타협 지향적 행동이 전략적으로 지배적인 행동으로 압축되면서 행동 다양성 상실
**시뮬레이션 신뢰성 문제** / Simulation Reliability Crisis: LLM 기반 사회·경제 시뮬레이션의 타당성에 대한 근본적 의문 제기 및 모델 선택 기준의 재구성 필요

articles.title: The illusory simplicity of the feedforward pass: evidence for the dynamical nature of stimulus encoding along the primate ventral stream
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.12825v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In studying primate vision, a large body of work focuses on the first feedforward sweep. During this initial time window, information is thought to pass through ventral stream regions in a stage-like fashion in an effort to extract high-level information from the retinal input. Consequently, electrophysiological analyses commonly focus on spatial response patterns, either by averaging data in time, or by applying decoders in a temporally local fashion. By analysing data recorded simultaneously across multiple arrays placed along the macaque ventr

articles.title: Brain-DiT: A Universal Multi-state fMRI Foundation Model with Metadata-Conditioned Pretraining
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.12683v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Current fMRI foundation models primarily rely on a limited range of brain states and mismatched pretraining tasks, restricting their ability to learn generalized representations across diverse brain states. We present \textit{Brain-DiT}, a universal multi-state fMRI foundation model pretrained on 349,898 sessions from 24 datasets spanning resting, task, naturalistic, disease, and sleep states. Unlike prior fMRI foundation models that rely on masked reconstruction in the raw-signal space or a latent space, \textit{Brain-DiT} adopts metadata-cond

articles.title: Speaker effects in language comprehension: An integrative model of language and speaker processing
articles.summary: arXiv:2412.07238v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: The identity of a speaker influences language comprehension through modulating perception and expectation. This review explores speaker effects and proposes an integrative model of language and speaker processing that integrates distinct mechanistic perspectives. We argue that speaker effects arise from the interplay between bottom-up perception-based processes, driven by acoustic-episodic memory, and top-down expectation-based processes, driven by a speaker model. We show that language and speaker processing are functionally integrated

articles.title: Characterizing higher-order representations through generative diffusion models explains human decoded neurofeedback performance
articles.summary: arXiv:2503.14333v4 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Brains construct not only "first-order" representations of the environment but also "higher-order" representations about those representations -- including higher-order uncertainty estimates that guide learning and adaptive behavior. Higher-order expectations about representational uncertainty -- i.e., learned through experience -- may play a key role in guiding behavior and learning, but their characterization remains empirically and theoretically challenging. Here, we introduce the Noise Estimation through Reinforcement-based Diffusio

articles.title: ‘We Have Never Been Individuals’
articles.summary: Take a long, deep breath in. Now slowly let it out. Each time you inhale, you’re drawing in oxygen from the plants around you. Once in your lungs, oxygen navigates the bloodstream, where it gets exchanged for carbon dioxide. With each exhale, you fill the air with carbon dioxide, the very substance that all these plants need for photosynthesis. What we breathe out, plants breathe in. What plants breathe out, we breathe in. The air you breathe is the collective breath of other living beings. You are immersed in the world. And the world is immersed in you. Your body hosts a remarkable diversity

articles.summary: Defusing a crisis after an ambassador hinted at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering demarches in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy with John Johnson, a retired diplomat who spent more than twenty years in the US Foreign Service. articles.title: ‘Looking Backward’ to the Future
articles.summary: I first saw the light in the city of Boston in the year 1857. “What!” you say. “Eighteen fifty-seven? That is an odd slip. He means nineteen fifty-seven, of course.” I beg pardon, but there is no mistake. It was about four in the afternoon of December the 26th, one day after Christmas, in the year 1857, not 1957, that I first breathed the east wind of Boston, which, I assure the reader, was at that remote period marked by the same penetrating quality characterizing it in the present year of grace, 2000. These statements seem so absurd on their face, especially when I add that I am a young man
